title: Sprint 54 Wave C: Extend PlacementBlock schema & parser with Facing + BlockState
description:
## Problem
Blocks with orientation (beds, doors, furnaces, stairs, slabs) place in wrong facing or fail entirely. The blueprint format has no way to specify orientation.

## Changes
1. **BlueprintSchema.cs**: Add `Facing` (north/south/east/west/up/down) and `BlockState` (optional string, e.g. "half=top") fields to `PlacementBlock`
2. **BlueprintParser.cs**: Parse `Facing: north` and `BlockState: half=top` from the markdown blueprint's per-block annotations (or a per-symbol facing map in the Legend section)

## Design
```csharp
// Before:
public record PlacementBlock(int X, int Y, int Z, string BlockId);

// After:
public record PlacementBlock(int X, int Y, int Z, string BlockId, 
    string? Facing = null, string? BlockState = null);
```

Where `Facing` is one of: north, south, east, west, up, down.

## Key files
- `Agent.Construction/BlueprintSchema.cs` — PlacementBlock record
- `Agent.Construction/BlueprintParser.cs` — ParseLayers / Legend parsing
- `Agent.Construction.Tests/` — BlueprintParserTests

## References
- Handoff: `Data/Pages/Handoffs/llm-replanning-core-round-5.md` Issue 1, 3
- AGENTS.md

comments.author: anonymous
comments.body:
**Dependencies**: None (first in Wave C chain)
**Depends on by**: TSK-0228, TSK-0229
**Handoff**: `Data/Pages/Handoffs/llm-replanning-core-round-5.md` Issues 1, 3

This is the foundational Wave C task. All downstream work (pipeline wiring, adapter changes) depends on `PlacementBlock` having `Facing` and `BlockState` fields.
